reasoning: Ariarne Titmus holds the 200m freestyle long course world record at 1:52.23, an extremely fast time set in June 2024. For her to lose the record by the resolution dates (1 week, 1 month, and 3 months from the cutoff), another swimmer (likely Mollie O'Callaghan, whose personal best is 1:52.48) would need to swim faster in a long course event. The period between November and January is typically dominated by short course (25m pool) competitions, making long course record-breaking opportunities rare. Furthermore, even if the record is broken, if Titmus breaks it herself, she would remain the record holder, which still fulfills the YES criteria. Therefore, the chance of another swimmer breaking her long course record within this 3-month off-peak window is very low.
batch_queries: According to Wikipedia, will Ariarne Titmus still hold the world record for 200m freestyle in long course (50 metres) swimming pools on each resolution date listed below?
